epics.title: Engine
epics.description: The autonomous engine itself: budget tracking, board, boot/wrap-up flow, CI cron. Permanent epic — always improvable.

epics.title: Dashboard
epics.description: The realtime PM dashboard. Permanent epic — always improvable.

epics.title: Phone Decomposition
epics.description: Landing page where a phone explodes into its internal layers as you scroll — 3D layer separation with annotations.

epics.title: Neon Arcade Game
epics.description: A playable single-file neon-styled mini game with juice: particles, screenshake, sound via WebAudio.

epics.title: Generative Art Piece
epics.description: Interactive canvas artwork producing a unique composition every visit; seeded randomness, exportable as PNG.

epics.title: Liquid Chrome Blob
epics.description: A reflective liquid-metal metaball that chases the cursor, merging and splitting like mercury

epics.title: Gravity Well Sandbox
epics.description: Drop massive bodies and watch glowing particles orbit under N-body gravity; a physics toy

epics.title: CRT Terminal OS
epics.description: A bootable fake retro OS with a working command prompt, scanlines, and hidden mini-commands

epics.title: Paper Storm
epics.description: A murmuration of thousands of origami birds swirling over a gradient dusk sky; the flock follows and dodges the cursor, folding/unfolding mid-flight

epics.title: Shatter Type
epics.description: Kinetic typography playground: giant letters built from physics particles explode as you type and magnetically reassemble into the new word

epics.title: Clockwork Garden
epics.description: A steampunk generative garden: brass gear-flowers bloom along spiral stems on hover, each procedurally unique, ticking like watch movements

epics.title: Sandfall Cathedral
epics.description: Falling-sand physics automaton that gradually builds a glowing stained-glass cathedral window

epics.title: Abyss Lantern
epics.description: Deep-sea bioluminescence scene: anglerfish lantern follows cursor, luring glowing creature swarms out of the dark

tasks.handoff: verified via chromium screenshot against live server

tasks.title: Cross-platform engine: browser + python detection for Windows/local runs
tasks.acceptance: engine scripts and skills work on Windows (chrome.exe path, python vs python3); documented in CLAUDE.md env notes; verified by running server + a screenshot locally.
tasks.handoff: lib.browser_path() + shot.py CLI + drive.py graceful chromedriver detection; CLAUDE.md env notes updated; verified: live dashboard screenshot on Windows

tasks.title: Dashboard: add-task form - queue a task from the UI, associate to an existing epic or create a new project epic
tasks.acceptance: Dashboard shows an Add Task form: title, size S/M, epic dropdown listing existing epics plus 'new project' (asks title+slug). server.py POST /api/task validates and writes it via board logic, placing it at a chosen queue position (default top). New task appears on the kanban within one refresh. Verified end-to-end in a real browser.
tasks.handoff: POST /api/task verified both paths (existing epic top-of-queue, new epic); form screenshot-verified in Chrome

tasks.handoff: Board now supports drag-to-move (status) + drag-to-reorder (next priority), click-to-open task modal with editable status/handoff/PR link, and PR links on cards. New POST /api/task/update endpoint + board.update_task().

tasks.title: dashboard improvement. at the moment nothing is really linked, it just look like that but is fake links
tasks.acceptance: ### Phase 1: Task Initialization * **Trigger:** A new task is assigned to you on the Dashboard. * **Action:**    1. Read the task details (title, description, initial tags).   2. Open a corresponding **GitHub Issue** in the repository.   3. Map the Dashboard tags to the appropriate **GitHub Labels (tags)** and apply them to the GitHub issue.   4. Update the Dashboard task with a link to the newly created GitHub Issue so they are linked.  ### Phase 2: Tag Synchronization * **Trigger:** A tag/label is added or removed on either the Dashboard or GitHub. * **Action:** Instantly sync the change. If a tag is added on GitHub, update the Dashboard task with that tag, and vice versa.  ### Phase 3: Task Completion & Merging * **Trigger:** You finish the work and are ready to close the task. * **Action:**   1. Create a **Pull Request / Merge Request (PR/MR)** for your branch.   2. Link the PR/MR to the GitHub Issue using close keywords (e.g., "Closes #IssueNumber").   3. Once the PR/MR is approved and successfully merged, ensure the GitHub Issue is closed.  ### Phase 4: Final Dashboard Update * **Trigger:** The GitHub Issue is closed and the PR/MR is merged. * **Action:**   1. Mark the Dashboard task as "Completed" / "Done".   2. Retrieve the merged **MR/PR Link** from GitHub.   3. Write/attach this MR/PR Link directly into the finished Dashboard task's designated field (e.g., description, comments, or custom link field).
tasks.handoff: Shipped real GitHub traceability. lib.commits_by_task maps each task to its shipping commit sha via git log, build_dashboard enriches tasks with commit, dashboard renders real commit-URL links on cards and detail modal. Fixes the fake-links complaint, verified in Chromium. Full 4-phase issue/PR/tag automation needs gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN not present here, filed as T27.

tasks.title: GitHub API automation: auto-create issues + PR close-keywords (needs gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN)
tasks.acceptance: When gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN is available: board.py claim opens a real GitHub issue and stores its number; board.py done opens/links a PR with 'Closes #N'; dashboard tag edits sync to GitHub labels both ways. Blocked in the current headless local env which has neither gh nor a token — the commit-link traceability from T26 is the token-free fallback.
tasks.handoff: issues auto-open on claim/in_progress, auto-close with commit link on done; github.py sync backfills; two-way label sync split to follow-up task

tasks.title: Dashboard: render budget history bars (used vs budget per past window, limit-hits marked red)
tasks.acceptance: dashboard/index.html renders budget.history (already in data.json) as a small bar strip under the gauge; limit-hit windows visually flagged; auto-raised budget visible; verified with screenshot
tasks.handoff: history bar strip under gauge: amber=overran/auto-raise, red=limit hit, dashed=current; tooltips with spend/budget; verified live screenshot

tasks.title: Dashboard: escape HTML in all injected strings
tasks.acceptance: titles, handoffs, acceptance, log msgs, learnings pass through an esc() helper before innerHTML; a task titled '' renders as text; board still renders correctly
tasks.handoff: esc() helper; all innerHTML sites escaped (board, gallery, feed, learnings, agent log, modal trace, form options); verified live with onerror-injection task

tasks.title: Dashboard: live tab title + favicon with budget % and verdict
tasks.acceptance: document.title shows e.g. '38% CONTINUE - CreativeWorld'; canvas-drawn favicon dot green/amber/red by verdict; updates each poll
tasks.handoff: tab title shows pct+verdict; canvas favicon dot green/amber/red with fill-level pie; verified via dump-dom

tasks.title: Dashboard: fix false IDLE when agent is quietly working
tasks.acceptance: when a task is in_progress and heartbeat is stale, panel shows 'working - quiet Xm' (amber) instead of 'IDLE (no agent)'; true idle unchanged
tasks.handoff: stale heartbeat + task in_progress now shows amber 'WORKING - quiet Xm' instead of false IDLE; verified with aged heartbeat

tasks.title: Per-task token cost attribution
tasks.acceptance: board.py claim/done snapshot window usage into the task (cost_start/cost); done tasks store real weighted cost; data.json exposes it; board cards + project gallery show cost; learnings sizing (S/M/L) can be checked against reals
tasks.handoff: claim/done snapshot total_usage into cost_start/cost; dashboard cards + gallery show measured cost; T32's own figure approximate (attribution landed mid-task), future tasks measure clean

tasks.title: Multi-agent safety: atomic state writes + claimed_by
tasks.acceptance: lib.save writes tmp+os.replace with a lockfile retry; claim stamps claimed_by/agent; second claim by another agent refuses; observed race (2 agents editing same project) can no longer corrupt state json
tasks.handoff: state_lock ctx mgr (O_EXCL lockfile, stale-break), atomic tmp+os.replace saves, claim transactional with claimed_by, server POST locked; verified: 8-proc hammer 200/200, second claim refused with holder name

tasks.title: heartbeat.py robustness: usage on bad args + trim log
tasks.acceptance: heartbeat.py status with no arg prints usage instead of traceback; log kept to last 100 entries; heartbeat.py show still works
tasks.handoff: usage on bad/missing args (no tracebacks), log trimmed to 100, opt() guards trailing flags; show works

tasks.title: Engine smoke test
tasks.acceptance: python engine/test_engine.py runs add-epic/add/claim/done/sync on a temp STATE dir + budget math sanity; exits 0; documented in CLAUDE.md work loop as pre-commit check for engine changes
tasks.handoff: test_engine.py: board lifecycle + lock + budget math on temp STATE, network stubbed; documented in CLAUDE.md git rules

tasks.title: GitHub labels two-way sync
tasks.acceptance: github.py sync also pulls: label/status edits made on GitHub issues update the board task (closed issue -> done, size label edit -> size); ran on boot; conflicts resolved board-wins
tasks.handoff: pull(): closed issue -> done, size labels -> size, claimed tasks immune (board wins); wired into brief (boot) and sync; verified with live GitHub edits on #16/#17

tasks.title: Rolling 5h budget window (match real subscription cap)
tasks.acceptance: lib budget math computes usage over the TRAILING 5h from all transcripts instead of discrete anchored windows; brief/budget_check/dashboard use it; discrete history kept for the bars; validated against the 04:22Z limit-hit-at-457k incident
tasks.handoff: used = max(anchored window, trailing-5h rolling sum); nap target follows binding measure; incident replay: rolling would have shown 52.1% vs anchored 7.9% at the limit hit; smoke test green

tasks.title: server.py: auto-restart on engine module change
tasks.acceptance: server watches mtimes of engine/*.py it imports and re-execs itself when they change (or serves via importlib.reload); editing build_dashboard.py no longer silently serves stale compose
tasks.handoff: mtime watcher thread re-execs server on engine/*.py change; verified live: compose edit appeared and disappeared in /api/state with zero manual restarts
